title: Fix std::randomize() in static function with static class members
body:
## Summary

`std::randomize()` called from a static class function, with a `with` clause referencing static class members, generates invalid C++ that fails to compile. Related to IEEE 1800-2017 18.11 (scope randomize).

## Current Behavior

The following pattern produces three C++ compilation errors:

```
  class instr_base;
    static instr_name_t allowed_instrs[$];
    static function instr_name_t get_rand_instr();
      instr_name_t name;
      std::randomize(name) with { name inside {allowed_instrs}; };
      return name;
    endfunction
  endclass
```

- has no member named '__VnoInFunc___VStdrand_*' — helper function placed on wrong class
- VlClassRef<class>{this} type mismatch — this pointer is package type, not class type
- has no member named '__PVT__allowed_instrs' — static member accessed via wrong class

## Expected Behavior

- std::randomize() in static function context with static member references compiles and runs correctly
- Randomized variable is constrained to the values in the static member collection

## Source
planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#67


PR is coming.
